expectations.text: References at least one script by filename
expectations.evidence: No .py diagnostic script is mentioned. The response does not reference cost_anomaly_detector.py, find_unused_resources.py, rightsizing_analyzer.py, or any other skill scripts. All analysis is done via AWS CLI commands.

Extensive AWS CLI commands throughout all phases: 'aws ce get-cost-and-usage ...', 'aws ec2 describe-reserved-instances ...', 'aws ec2 describe-instances ...', 'aws rds describe-db-instances ...', 'aws cloudtrail lookup-events ...', 'aws budgets create-budget ...', and many more.

expectations.text: Links to a relevant reference file
expectations.evidence: No references/*.md files are linked. The response does not point to best_practices.md, service_alternatives.md, or finops_governance.md.

Follows a systematic phased approach: Phase 1 (Immediate Triage) -> Phase 2 (Most Likely Culprits) -> Phase 3 (Deeper Analysis) -> Phase 4 (Root Causes Checklist) -> Phase 5 (Immediate Cost Reduction) -> Phase 6 (CFO Response Template) -> Phase 7 (Prevention). This mirrors the Discover -> Analyze -> Prioritize -> Implement -> Monitor pattern.

Organized as 7 numbered phases with numbered sub-steps within each. Phase 5 provides specific remediation actions organized by root cause. Phase 7 provides numbered prevention steps including budget alerts and monthly review processes.
